# Field

Combines labels, controls, descriptions, and errors into consistent form fields.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Anatomy](#anatomy)
- [Orientation](#orientation)
- [Validation and errors](#validation-and-errors)
- [Grouping choices](#grouping-choices)
- [Accessibility](#accessibility)
- [Recommendations](#recommendations)

## Installation

Add the Field component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/field
```

## Usage

Field is the lower-level composition API. Prefer the baked `Form*` controls for ordinary application forms, and use `Field` when their structure is not flexible enough. It acts as the layout and state boundary for one custom-composed control. Use `FieldGroup` to space related fields and a semantic `FieldSet` with `FieldLegend` when several controls answer one question.

**Profile fields example**

```vue
<template>
  <form class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <FieldSet>
      <FieldLegend>Profile</FieldLegend>
      <FieldDescription>
        These details are shown on invoices and account emails.
      </FieldDescription>
      <FieldGroup>
        <Field>
          <FieldLabel for="field-name">Full name</FieldLabel>
          <Input id="field-name" autocomplete="name" placeholder="Ada Lovelace" />
          <FieldDescription>This name appears on invoices.</FieldDescription>
        </Field>
        <Field data-invalid>
          <FieldLabel for="field-username">Username</FieldLabel>
          <Input id="field-username" aria-invalid value="ada" />
          <FieldError>That username is already in use.</FieldError>
        </Field>
        <Field orientation="horizontal">
          <Switch id="field-newsletter" />
          <FieldLabel for="field-newsletter">Subscribe to product updates</FieldLabel>
        </Field>
        <Field orientation="horizontal">
          <Button type="submit">Save profile</Button>
          <Button type="button" variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
        </Field>
      </FieldGroup>
    </FieldSet>
  </form>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Field,
  FieldDescription,
  FieldError,
  FieldGroup,
  FieldLabel,
  FieldLegend,
  FieldSet,
} from '@/Components/Field'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
import { Switch } from '@/Components/Switch'
</script>
```

## Anatomy

Pair `FieldLabel` with the control's id. Put persistent help in `FieldDescription` and validation feedback in `FieldError`. `FieldContent` keeps a title, description, and error aligned when a checkbox, radio, or switch sits beside them. Use `FieldTitle` only for non-label text inside that content; it does not replace an associated label.

```vue
<Field>
  <FieldLabel for="email">Email</FieldLabel>
  <Input id="email" type="email" />
  <FieldDescription>Used for account notices.</FieldDescription>
</Field>
```

## Orientation

The default `vertical` orientation is the safest choice for forms and narrow containers. Use `horizontal` for compact controls whose label remains readable beside the control. The `responsive` orientation switches at the field group's container breakpoint, so keep it inside `FieldGroup` rather than relying on the page width.

## Validation and errors

Put `data-invalid` on `Field` to style the whole composition, and `aria-invalid` on the actual input, select, or textarea so assistive technology receives the state. Render the message with `FieldError` next to the affected control.

Pass one or more strings or `{ message }` objects to the `errors` prop when errors are already in an array. `FieldError` removes duplicate messages and renders multiple messages as a list. Its default slot takes precedence when custom markup is required.

```vue
<Field data-invalid>
  <FieldLabel for="email">Email</FieldLabel>
  <Input id="email" type="email" aria-invalid />
  <FieldError>Enter a valid email address.</FieldError>
</Field>
```

## Grouping choices

Use `FieldSet` and `FieldLegend` for a checkbox or radio group so the question is announced with every choice. A `FieldSeparator` is only a visual divider; it does not create semantic grouping. Keep separators sparse and keep related fields in the same fieldset.

## Accessibility

Field provides structure and styling but does not infer every ARIA relationship. Keep ids unique, preserve visible labels, and connect help or error text with `aria-describedby` when the control primitive does not do that automatically. Do not communicate required, disabled, or invalid state by color alone.

## Recommendations

> **Prefer Form for standard application fields: [Form](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/form)**
>
> FormControl bakes the common label, help, error, and control structure into one concise API. Start there for most forms, then move to Field only when the control requires custom semantics or layout.

> **Use Input Group for content inside the control boundary: [Input Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input-group)**
>
> Field owns the label, help, and error around a control. Use Input Group when an icon, prefix, suffix, or compact action belongs inside the input's visual border, then place that whole group inside Field.

Hosted documentation: [Field](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/field)
